texto: Twas a sheep not a lamb that strayed awayIn the parable Jesus told,A grown-up sheep that strayed awayFrom the ninety and nine in the fold.And why for the sheep should we seekAnd earnestly hope and pray?Because there is danger when sheep go wrong;They lead the lambs astray.Lambs will follow the sheep, you know,Wherever the sheep may stray.When sheep go wrong, it won’t take longTil the lambs are as wrong as they.And so with the sheep we earnestly pleadFor the sake of the lambs today,For when sheep are lost, what a terrible costThe lambs will have to pay!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de C.C. Miller que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Twas a sheep not a lamb that strayed awayIn the parable Jesus told,A grown-up sheep that strayed …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, poem, sheep, com foco em reflexao.
